channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Former GOP operative warns Trump’s handling of Iran conflict could lead to further wars
item.description: A former Republican political operative, Steve Schmidt, has warned that the United States may be entering a period of increased global instability following what he described as an unsuccessful confli...

item.title: Arthur Fery Advances to Third Round as Last British Player in Wimbledon Singles
item.description: Arthur Fery has emerged as the last British competitor remaining in the Wimbledon singles draw after a series of early exits for his compatriots. The 23-year-old wildcard secured his place in the thir...

item.title: Leaked video reveals internal conflict at Michael Flynn’s nonprofit tied to MAGA and anti-trafficking work
item.description: A newly obtained video reviewed by The Bulwark reportedly captures a heated confrontation involving individuals connected to former Trump national security adviser Michael Flynn’s nonprofit organizati...

item.title: DEA to temporarily schedule kratom-derived compound 7-OH as a Schedule I substance
item.description: The U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) plans to temporarily classify 7-hydroxymitragynine (7-OH), a potent compound derived from the kratom plant, as a Schedule I controlled substance. This cl...

item.title: 1940 Photograph Shows Baby Lamb Resting Beside Sleeping Boy in Wartime England
item.description: This article highlights a vintage photograph taken in 1940 in rural England during the early period of World War II. The image depicts a tender and peaceful moment between a sleeping young boy and a b...

item.title: IQM goes public via SPAC while warning that large-scale quantum computing success remains uncertain
item.description: IQM, a Finland-based full-stack quantum computing company and spinout from Aalto University, has gone public through a SPAC merger on Nasdaq at an estimated valuation of around $1.9 billion. Despite t...

item.title: Menswear critic analyzes suit fit issues of White House official Stephen Miller at Washington event
item.description: A Raw Story report covers commentary from menswear expert Derek Guy, who analyzed the suit worn by White House deputy chief of staff Stephen Miller during a public appearance in Washington, D.C. The e...

item.title: Dancer nearly struck by falling stage panel during Freedom 250 rehearsal in Washington, D.C.
item.description: During rehearsals for the Freedom 250 July 4 celebration in Washington, D.C., a stage malfunction nearly caused a serious accident when a large structural panel detached and fell toward performers. Ac...

item.title: Jeanine Pirro Faces Criticism Over Felony Indictment of Former Olympian in Reflecting Pool Damage Case
item.description: U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia Jeanine Pirro is facing public criticism following the felony indictment of former Olympic canoeist David Carter Hearn, 67, in connection with alleged damage...

item.title: Economist warns of slowdown in U.S. job growth following weak June labor report
item.description: An economist has raised concerns about the state of the U.S. labor market following the release of a new Department of Labor report showing weaker-than-expected job growth in June. According to the re...

item.title: Justice Department mistakenly discloses sealed special counsel report related to Trump classified documents case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) has inadvertently released a sealed portion of former special counsel Jack Smith’s investigative report concerning former President Donald Trump. The released mate...

item.title: Claire Boscher’s Frozen Flower Pops Blend Flowers and Ice in Surreal AI-Assisted Art
item.description: Claire Boscher presents a visually striking series titled “Frozen Flower Pops,” an artistic exploration that transforms floral imagery into surreal, ice-bound compositions. Known for her ability to ca...

item.title: Lewis Hamilton speaks about his relationship with Kim Kardashian during Silverstone appearance ahead of British Grand Prix
item.description: Lewis Hamilton made a public reference to his relationship with Kim Kardashian during a fan stage appearance at Silverstone ahead of the British Grand Prix. Speaking alongside fellow Formula 1 drivers...

item.title: UDA revises party constitution and seeks public input ahead of 2027 elections
item.description: The United Democratic Alliance (UDA), which is the ruling party in Kenya, has announced amendments to its party constitution as part of its ongoing internal reforms ahead of the 2027 General Election....

item.title: ECOWAS Court orders Ghana to pay damages over unlawful passport revocation case
item.description: The ECOWAS Community Court of Justice has ordered the Republic of Ghana to pay N21 million in damages to a Ghanaian-born woman, Mary Omerere, after ruling that her fundamental rights were violated whe...

item.title: Conservative backlash erupts over NYC heat wave energy-saving air conditioning guidance
item.description: The article describes a political and cultural controversy sparked by energy conservation recommendations issued during a severe heat wave in New York City. Mayor Zohran Mamdani advised residents to h...

item.title: Government launches Makambako–Songea road upgrade to strengthen southern transport corridor
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has officially launched the construction of a new section of the Makambako–Songea road, a key transport route linking the Njombe and Ruvuma regions. The road, which has serv...

item.title: ASUU issues strike warning affecting 11 state universities over unpaid allowances
item.description: The Academic Staff Union of Universities (ASUU) has issued a fresh warning of a possible indefinite strike that could disrupt academic activities in 11 state-owned universities across Nigeria. The aff...

item.title: Raja Jackson sentenced to jail and probation after assault during pro-wrestling event
item.description: Raja Jackson, the son of former UFC champion Quinton “Rampage” Jackson, has been taken into custody in Los Angeles County to serve a court-approved 90-day jail sentence related to a violent incident a...

item.title: Federal and state investigators examine alleged 'ghost candidate' case in Alaska Senate race
item.description: Federal and state authorities are investigating a contested U.S. Senate race in Alaska involving two candidates with the same name, Dan Sullivan. According to reporting cited in the article, the Alask...
